pr.title: fix: use -p flag and positional prompt for Claude CLI

comments.body: You have reached your Codex usage limits for code reviews. You can see your limits in the [Codex usage dashboard](https://chatgpt.com/codex/settings/usage).

## Summary of Changes Hello, I'm Gemini Code Assist[^1]! I'm currently reviewing this pull request and will post my feedback shortly. In the meantime, here's a summary to help you and other reviewers quickly get up to speed! This pull request addresses a critical issue where Claude Code managed workflows were failing due to an incompatibility with the Claude CLI's argument parsing. By updating the command construction to use the correct `-p` flag and positional arguments for prompts, it ensures that these workflows can execute successfully. The changes also guarantee that the CLI always operates in non-interactive mode, which is essential for automated environments. ### Highlights * **CLI Argument Correction**: The `--prompt` flag for the Claude CLI has been replaced with the `-p` flag, and the instruction is now passed as a positional argument, aligning with the updated Claude CLI v2.1.50 behavior. * **Non-Interactive Mode Enforcement**: The `-p` flag, which enables non-interactive mode, is now always appended to the Claude CLI command, as it is required for managed subprocess runs. * **Test Updates**: Three assertions in `test_remaining_strategies.py` were updated to reflect the new command structure, ensuring all 27 strategy tests continue to pass. 🧠 **New Feature in Public Preview:** You can now enable **Memory** to help **Gemini Code Assist** learn from your team's feedback. This makes future code reviews more consistent and personalized to your project's style. **Click [here](https://codeassist.google/code-review/login) to enable Memory in your admin console.**
Using Gemini Code Assist
The full guide for Gemini Code Assist can be found on our [documentation page](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-github-code), here are some quick tips. Invoking Gemini You can request assistance from Gemini at any point by creating a comment using either `/gemini ` or `@gemini-code-assist `. Below is a summary of the supported commands on the current page. Feature | Command | Description --- | --- | --- Code Review | `/gemini review` | Performs a code review for the current pull request in its current state. Pull Request Summary | `/gemini summary` | Provides a summary of the current pull request in its current state. Comment | @gemini-code-assist | Responds in comments when explicitly tagged, both in pull request comments and review comments. Help | `/gemini help` | Displays a list of available commands. Customization To customize Gemini Code Assist for GitHub experience, repository maintainers can create a configuration file and/or provide a custom code review style guide (such as PEP-8 for Python) by creating and adding files to a `.gemini/` folder in the base of the repository. Detailed instructions can be found [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Limitations & Feedback Gemini Code Assist may make mistakes. Please leave feedback on any instances where its feedback is incorrect or counter productive. You can react with :thumbsup: and :thumbsdown: on @gemini-code-assist comments. If you're interested in giving your feedback about your experience with Gemini Code Assist for GitHub and other Google products, sign up [here](https://google.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_2cyuGuTWsEw84yG).
[^1]: Review the [Privacy Notices](https://policies.google.com/privacy), [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy), [Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms), and learn how to configure Gemini Code Assist in GitHub [here](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/customize-gemini-behavior-github). Gemini can make mistakes, so double check it and [use code with caution](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/13505487).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

This change correctly passes the instruction as a positional argument. However, this is redundant with the `prepare_workspace` method in the same class, which writes the same instruction to `CLAUDE.md`. The documentation in the base class also indicates that `CLAUDE.md` is the intended mechanism for passing instructions to the Claude runtime.

This duplication can lead to confusion and potential bugs. The instruction should be passed via a single, clear mechanism. If using a positional argument is the correct approach now, the file-writing logic in `prepare_workspace` should be removed to eliminate the redundancy.

## Code Review

This pull request updates the Claude CLI command construction to use a positional argument for instructions and includes the `-p` flag for non-interactive mode. The reviewer noted a potential redundancy where instructions are now passed as a positional argument, but also written to `CLAUDE.md` by the `prepare_workspace` method, suggesting that one of these mechanisms should be removed to avoid confusion and potential bugs.
